Damone Washington — Portfolio

AI Researcher & Software Developer · NC-LSAMP Student Researcher · WSSU Class of 2027

Home About Projects Resume GitHub LinkedIn

About Damone

I grew up driven by a curiosity for how systems think and communicate — which led me to computer science. I started at Forsyth Technical Community College, earned my Associate’s degree in 2024, and transferred to Winston-Salem State University to complete my Bachelor’s. I graduate in 2027.

My work sits at the intersection of software engineering and artificial intelligence. I’m particularly drawn to graph theory, knowledge representation, and building tools that extract meaning from complex, connected data.

Research

Since September 2024, I’ve been a Student Researcher with the North Carolina Louis Stokes Alliance for Minority Participation (NC-LSAMP) — an NSF-funded program supporting underrepresented students in STEM research. This role has sharpened my ability to approach problems with scientific rigor and contribute to initiatives beyond the classroom.

Education

Winston-Salem State University Bachelor of Arts, Computer and Information Sciences · 2024 – 2027

Forsyth Technical Community College Associate of Applied Science, Computer and Information Sciences · 2022 – 2024

Coursework

Data Structures  ·  Algorithms  ·  Databases  ·  Operating Systems  ·  Web Development  ·  Artificial Intelligence

What I’m Looking For

I’m actively seeking internship opportunities in software engineering, AI/ML, or data science. I bring hands-on research experience, a Google AI certification, and a demonstrated ability to design and build complex systems from scratch — including graph-based knowledge systems and web crawlers.

If you’re building something interesting, let’s talk.
